calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2013 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions, Striking Defts Answer And Entering Default; Request For Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Feb-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.

RULING: PLAINTIFF CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUREAU, INC. DBA CAB-LCF's Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions, Striking Defendant's Answer And Entering Default; Request For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The docket shows that Defendant Loomis Group, Inc. filed a notice of assignment for the benefit of creditors with the Court on December 6, 2012. The stipulation between Plaintiff's Counsel and Defendant's counsel filed with the Court on January 29, 2012 also establishes that Plaintiff is aware that Defendant's Assignee for the Benefit of Creditors is Credit Management Association. The Proof of Service of the motion for terminating sanctions only establishes service on Defendant's counsel who, as described in the January 29, 2012 stipulation, is no longer authorized to act on behalf of Defendant. Credit Management Association, as the "legal representative" of the assignor and as trustee for all the creditors, is charged with the duty to defend the property in its hands against adverse claims, and was entitled to notice of the motion. Credit Managers Ass'n v. Nat'l Indep. Bus. Alliance, 162 Cal. App. 3d 1166, 1172 (Cal. App. 2d Dist. 1984)(granting relief to assignee for the benefit of creditors from default). (Court to prepare order.) Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Docs; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P&A; Declaration Of Kenneth J. Freed
Off Calendar Dec-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
PLAINTIFF CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUREAU, INC. DBA CAB-LCF'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Monetary Sanctions - There being no opposition filed, the motion is granted. Defendant shall provide full and complete responses, without objections, to Plaintiff's request for production of documents, set number 1, within 20 days of the date of this order. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $640.00, to be paid by Defendant to Plaintiff's counsel within 20 days of the date of this order. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. Pro Tem Judge: Jim Weixel, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/jpt
